text: The bank approved the loan application after reviewing the applicant's credit history.
sense_definitions:
Sense 1: A financial institution that accepts deposits and channels the money into lending activities.
Sense 2: The land alongside or sloping down to a river or lake.
Sense 3: A long pile or heap of something.
Sense 4: A set of similar things arranged in a row.

text: She decided to run for the local council seat in the upcoming election.
sense_definitions:
Sense 1: To move at a speed faster than a walk, with both feet off the ground.
Sense 2: To compete as a candidate in a political election.
Sense 3: To operate or manage a business or organization.
Sense 4: To flow or extend in a particular direction.

text: The bright light from the lamp cast long shadows across the room.
sense_definitions:
Sense 1: The natural agent that stimulates sight and makes things visible.
Sense 2: Having little weight; not heavy.
Sense 3: Not dense or dark in color; pale.
Sense 4: To set on fire; to ignite.

text: The company had to address several critical issues before the product launch.
sense_definitions:
Sense 1: The details of where a person lives or an organization is located.
Sense 2: To deal with or give attention to a matter or problem.
Sense 3: A formal speech delivered to an audience.
Sense 4: To write the name and location of the intended recipient on an envelope.

text: The stem of the plant had grown several inches since last week.
sense_definitions:
Sense 1: The main body or stalk of a plant.
Sense 2: To originate or be caused by something.
Sense 3: To stop or restrict the flow of something.
Sense 4: The main part of a word to which affixes are added.

text: The pitcher threw a sharp curve that completely fooled the batter.
sense_definitions:
Sense 1: A container with a handle and spout used for holding and pouring liquids.
Sense 2: The player who throws the ball to the batter in baseball.
Sense 3: A large stone used in paving.
Sense 4: A person who pitches goods or ideas.

text: The interest rate on savings accounts has remained stable throughout the quarter.
sense_definitions:
Sense 1: A feeling of curiosity or concern about something.
Sense 2: Money paid regularly for the use of money lent or for delaying repayment.
Sense 3: A stake or share in an undertaking or business.
Sense 4: The advantage or benefit of a person or group.

text: She had to spring over the puddle to avoid getting her shoes wet.
sense_definitions:
Sense 1: The season after winter and before summer.
Sense 2: To jump or leap suddenly.
Sense 3: A natural flow of water from the ground.
Sense 4: A resilient device that returns to its original shape after compression.

text: The court ruled in favor of the defendant, dismissing all charges due to insufficient evidence.
sense_definitions:
Sense 1: A tribunal presided over by a judge for administering justice.
Sense 2: An area marked out for playing sports such as tennis or basketball.
Sense 3: The residence or establishment of a sovereign.
Sense 4: To try to win the favor or affection of someone.

text: The organ played a beautiful melody during the church service on Sunday morning.
sense_definitions:
Sense 1: A part of the body that performs a specific function, such as the heart or liver.
Sense 2: A large musical instrument with pipes, played using keyboards and pedals.
Sense 3: A newspaper or periodical serving a particular cause or organization.
Sense 4: An institution or establishment regarded as serving a particular purpose.
